verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted as a latent pro-cytokine (LAP-TGFβ1) but is covalently disulfide-tethered via Cys33 of LAP to the transmembrane partners GARP/LRRC32 (on platelets and activated FOXP3+ Tregs) and LRRC33 (on myeloid cells), producing a wash-resistant surface-displayed complex assembled intracellularly and co-trafficked to the PM. Anti-LAP and anti-GARP:TGFβ1 antibodies (e.g., livmoniplimab) clinically engage this surface pool. Soluble/ECM (LTBP-bound) pools also exist, so display is context-restricted to GARP+/LRRC33+ cells.
